zotero.abstractNote: Interactive Reinforcement Learning (IntRL) allows agents to learn from human feedback, which can significantly speed up the learning process. However, most existing approaches rely on a single feedback channel, such as scalar rewards or demonstrations. In this paper, we propose a multi-channel IntRL framework that allows an agent to learn from multiple types of human feedback, including scalar rewards, demonstrations, and corrections. We introduce a novel approach to combine these feedback channels by learning a shared representation of the human's intent. We evaluate our approach on a set of sequential tasks and show that it outperforms single-channel IntRL methods in terms of learning speed and final performance.
